description.value:
As a Geisha, skilled in the fine arts and beauty, you are not expected to fight directly. Instead, a special seal, unique to the Geisha clan, binds a bodyguard to your service.
These individuals may be slaves, debt payers, devoted protectors, or even fans and admirers, each fulfilling their role in different ways.
These protectors fight on your behalf, often treated decently by the Geisha clan, as they are regarded as the guardians of the Geisha's wellbeing.
The seal placed upon them grants them access to special abilities, but also imposes certain restrictions.
The seal can only be removed by a matron of the Geisha clan, and only with the Geisha's approval, or if the bodyguard has fulfilled their debt or duty to the clan.
Your bodyguard can take one of the following forms:

  • Brute: A heavily built and resilient bodyguard trained in the arts of Taijutsu, capable of absorbing significant punishment.
  • Warrior: A skilled Bukijutsu practitioner who excels at fighting with various type of weapons, perfectly capable of handling any combat on your behalf.
  • Gentleman: A proficient Ninjutsu user with a wide range of techniques and their own release, which may differ from your own.
  • Flamboyant: A charming bodyguard skilled in the arts of Genjutsu, able to manipulate others in line with your wishes.

Regardless of the type of bodyguard you choose, all of them share the following traits and abilities:

  • Loyalty Seal: The bodyguard is forbidden from harming any Geisha clan member, especially their own Geisha, whether willingly or by accident. Should the Geisha permit it, they may temporarily allow their bodyguard to harm other clan members in cases of emergency.
    Any attempt to hurt a Geisha clan member or their own Geisha results in excruciating pain, rendering the bodyguard incapable of acting for that turn if forced, or for 1 minute if the action was willingly hostile towards any Geisha clan member.

  • Life Thread: A bodyguard who falls in battle does not die. Instead, they are transported to Hanamachi (The Flower Town), where they will be healed and restored to continue serving their Geisha.
    After a long rest, the Geisha can summon the bodyguard back to their side.

  • Call of Duty: As a reaction, the Geisha can summon or teleport her bodyguard to shield her from harm, as long as they are within 30 feet.
    The bodyguard interposes themselves between the Geisha and any incoming attacks or harmful effects, granting her total cover.
    Even area-of-effect (AoE) attacks will be entirely absorbed by the bodyguard, and the effects or damage are recalculated using the bodyguard's stats.

  • The Most Important: Jutsu with a range of "self", possessed by the bodyguard, that are meant to protect, heal, or reduce damage (Add AC, Add saving throws bonuses, Add Temp HP or similar) can instead be applied to the Geisha (not both) if she is within 30 feet of her bodyguard.
  • Forced Dependency: Due to the special seal binding the bodyguard to the Geisha, they are prohibited from learning or using summoning jutsu or any jutsu that summons other creatures.

Credit: Fate Samurai Remnant

 

 

The bodyguard have the following stats and mechanics:

  • Shares your level.

  • Proficient in all saving throws.

  • Can only learn jutsu for their specialty (taijutsu, bukijutsu, ninjutsu (chosen element + non-elemental), or genjutsu) and they start with 2 for the Brute and Warrior, 3 for the Flamboyant, and 5 for the Gentleman.
    Every 2 levels, they learn an additional jutsu they qualify for.

  • Their HP, Jutsu slot, and AC are calculated based on their type, and they always get max HP per level.

  • At levels 4, 8, 12, 16, and 20, they gain an additional 2 ability score improvements.

  • To recover HP and chakra, they use your hit and chakra dice (Recover the same amount of HP and 1 jutsu slot per chakra die used). Both of you benefit from their usage.

Use the character sheets at the end of this clan to see the bodyguard stats for each type.
The bodyguard only possesses a reaction by themselves; you need to give your own action or bonus action for them to also possess one.
The bodyguard does not have their own initiative; instead, they share yours and can take their own actions when you give them to him.
In case the Geisha is ever taken down before their bodyguard, the bodyguard will gain an action and bonus action, which they will use to help or save the Geisha at all costs.

Starting at level 7, your bodyguard gains an independent bonus action. If you decide to give him your bonus action, then his bonus action becomes an action.
Each type gains an additional skill here detailed:

  • Brute: Resilience: When coming back using the "Come My Champion" trait, the brute comes back with 1 less exhaustion point and will lose the other 2 on the next long rest.
  • Warrior: Fervor: When coming back using the "Come My Champion" trait, the warrior can immediately make 1 free jutsu without using one of his jutsu slots as a free action.
  • Gentleman: Mana Recharge: When coming back using the "Come My Champion" trait, the Gentleman also recovers up to half of his proficiency modifier jutsu slots (rounded up).
  • Flamboyant: Flashy Entrance: When coming back using the "Come My Champion" trait, the flamboyant comes back with a flashy show. All creatures within 25 ft must make a Charisma saving throw against his Genjutsu DC or be charmed by him and the Geisha until the end of their next turn.

At level 11, the bodyguard becomes much more skilled and independent.
The bodyguard gains an action and you can only gift him a bonus action. If the Geisha is unconscious while he is still up, he gains an additional action per turn. Each type gains an additional skill here detailed:

  • Brute: Anger Management: Your basic attack increases by 1d12 and your "Angry Shockwave" now deals more damage by 2d12. Its range becomes a radius and inflicts 2 ranks of Bruised on a failure or 1 on a success.

  • Warrior: Superior Mastery: You can attack one extra time per turn if you ever used your attack action.
    Your "For My Lady" now deals 5 times your weapon damage instead of 3, and you can move up to half your speed, hitting anyone along the way, dealing 2 ranks of Bleeding on a failure and 1 on a success.

  • Gentleman: Raw Power: Your chakra bolt now can hit targets up to 50 ft away and deals 1d10.
    Additionally, it deals half of the damage dealt to anyone within 5 ft of the original target.
    Your "Chakra Explosion" now becomes 25 ft, deals more damage by 2d8, and inflicts 2 ranks of Concussed on a failure or 1 on a success.

  • Flamboyant: Flashy Improvement: You can now create martial weapons as long as they do not have the heavy or two-handed property with your "Illusory Weaponry" and its critical range is now 19.
    Your flashy presence extends up to 50 ft, It adds 2 ranks of Dazzled on a failure and 1 on a success.

 

 

At level 15, the bodyguard becomes fully independent and knows exactly what to do to protect his mistress. He gains his own turn with both a bonus action and action, and you no longer need to give him any actions.
If the Geisha is unconscious, he gains a second turn in combat immediately after the first.
Each type gains a final skill here detailed:

  • Brute: For My Flower: The brute can give up all of his HP to bring the Geisha back to life if she was killed (the Brute must be above half HP and the Geisha must have died in the last minute), this works also if she was simply downed.
    She will gain half of the HP the brute sacrificed, and the extra that goes above her max HP will be considered temporary HP.
    The berserker will be out of commission until a full rest is completed.

  • Warrior: Complete Mastery: For each weapon attack you make, you can make an additional weapon attack for free (including the ones made as a reaction).
  • Gentleman: Chakra Exchange: The Gentleman can take and give chakra with the Geisha. Each jutsu slot is 5 chakra to give or take, this can be done once per round as a free action (no limit to how many at a time).

  • Flamboyant: Flashy Surprise (Recharge 5-6): As an action, the bodyguard can target up to your proficiency modifier in creatures within 50 ft of you, making one genjutsu attack for all creatures.
    Those hit will take your proficiency modifier in d6 psychic damage as shadow creatures attack them.

Bodyguards are NPCs created with the DM and can have their own life, backstory, objectives, and behavior.
Work with your DM to create a special character that complements your story and allows your DM to roleplay the bodyguard outside of combat as a normal NPC would. Together, decide how the bodyguard came to serve your clan and explore his personal choices, motivations, and role within your narrative.
